prompt:
A medium-shot authentic daily life photograph, natural candid moment, of an East Asian young woman standing at the corner of a long interior corridor. 85mm lens, waist-up framing, shallow depth of field. The corridor behind her recedes into soft depth — wooden panelling on one side, white plaster on the other, afternoon light arriving distantly at the far end.
East Asian young woman in her early 20s. Almond-shaped eyes, elongated outer corners, gaze directed slightly sideways down the corridor's length. Straight delicate nose bridge. Skin tone fair beige, calm in the quiet of the hallway. Skin subsurface scattering visible under directional light, specular micro-highlights on cheekbones and nose ridge, fine foundation powder grain perceptible.
She wears a charcoal grey silk qipao with gold phoenix embroidery — the gold thread dulled and settled, more like old lacquer than new gilt. Mandarin collar, short sleeves, fitted silhouette. She stands still at the corner without purpose, one shoulder near the wall, gaze carrying down the corridor as if listening. The corridor holds nothing else — no art, no furniture, just the texture of the wall and the long pull of the floor. Two or three stray hairs displaced by faint air movement, natural unplanned imperfection, not geometrically symmetrical.
Diffuse natural light arrives from the corridor's distant end, catching the near side of her face and the left shoulder of the qipao, leaving the rest in soft shadow. The charcoal silk absorbs the dim light with quiet weight. Mood: a pause between one room and the next. Subtle ISO 400 film grain in shadow areas, photographic noise texture not CG render smoothness. Aspect ratio 9:16. No watermark, no text overlay, not cartoon, not digitally painted, not illustration, not anime.
